The '''Brownies''' were a diminutive, long-lived species from [[Andowyne]] that defied most scientifc classifications. While they appeared [[Magic/Legends|magical]], Brownies possessed little to no affinity for [[The Force/Legends|the Force]], though they respected the supernatural and showed reverence for the fey folk with whom they shared the forests of Andowyne.<ref name="Databank" />

As a species, Brownies tended to live in communities they referred to as kingdoms—a testament to their apparent inflated opinion of their place in the world—in small numbers, and appeared to be a predominantly male society as female Brownies went uncataloged along with the species' gender roles and reproductive systems.<ref name="Databank" />

Despite their small stature, they were resourceful and extremely loyal, prostrating themselves before, and willing do to the bidding of [[Willow (film)|Cherlindrea]]. Coupled with a tendency for pranks, however, the Brownies were considered to be pests and vermin by the planet's other species.<ref name="Databank" /> Some Brownies were known to have kidnapped a [[Daikini]] [[Elora Danan|baby]].<ref name="Danan DB">{{DB|updates|401/character/eloradanan|Danan, Elora}}</ref><ref name="Madmartigan DB">{{DB|updates|401/character/madmartigan|Madmartigan}}</ref><ref name="Thorn DB">{{DB|updates|401/character/thorndrumheller|Drumheller, Thorn}}</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
On [[April 1]], [[2006]], [[StarWars.com]] updated its [[Databank (original)|Databank]] with elements from the film [[Willow (film)|''Willow'']] and its spin-off novels, indicating that they would be added to the ''[[Star Wars]]'' continuity. Elaborate databank entries were written which attempted to plausibly fit the material into the history of the ''[[Star Wars]]'' universe. However, two days later, it was revealed to be an [[Wikipedia:April Fools' Day|April Fools' joke]], and thus non-[[canon]].<ref name="Shadow DB">{{DB|updates|401/news20060401.html|The Shadow War Chronicles}}</ref>

In the film ''Willow'', the Brownies were portrayed by [[Kevin Pollak]]<ref name="Pollack DB">{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/member/insideronline/6/indexp5.html|text=Kevin Pollack: Playing Small, But Thinking Big}}</ref> and [[Wikipedia:Rick Overton|Rick Overton]].<ref name="Rick Website">{{WebCite|url=http://www.rickoverton.net/credits.php|text=Rick Overton Film & TV Credits|work=Rick Overton|archivedate=20080705020611}}</ref>

The [[Patitite/Legends|Patitite]] species from the ''[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars]]'' episode "[[Nomad Droids]]" were intended to be similar to the Brownies.<ref name="Willow">{{SW|url=video/questions-answered-nass-orphne-and-willow-the-clone-wars|text=Questions Answered: Nass, Orphne and Willow}}</ref>
